Title IX: A Programmer's Perspective

Fantastic Preys and Where to Find Them

Over-simplified, not yet scientifically mature, and ego - a call for leveraging AI on this topic, under the Cornell environment, a Galapagos of human civilization who served as a lab in past events like COVID.

June 25, 2025 marked the start the primary hearing of Liren Chen's case which the Tsinghua University alum, Google software engineer punched his wife, a peer from the same school and workplace, to death. Shortly, the case raised grieviance among the group of people very much like myself, my peers, victims and prepatrators in my own story and those I have witnessed, who self identify as high academic achievers in China of different social economic status, struggle to immigrate to the US to settle in high paying jobs like software engineers or some sort of honorable placements, had lost some to all degrees of human nature in this siricl, while some people I witnessed had never had a chance to be a high achiever at early stage but to be joked by the society for being lazy. Without any further perfectionism, I turn in this assignment, letting go anything else like

  • 'Polish this to a well cited scientific portfolio' (but I indeed cited everything just pasting the hyperlinks directly),
  • 'find good translation for the sources other than English and organize them well (try let the AI do that)' to
  • 'Beg the school to look at me once again to recongize me as a potential genieous researcher, not a loser'
    • or
  • 'Beg forgiveness for past academic stumbles, some errectic interuptions when I was at school'
    • , and
  • 'only having this humble level of writing and web design at this stage of career'
.

Abstract

Hi there, this is some anonyous Cornell alum from Computing and Information School. She (me) had lost a significant amount of academic and professsional placement, had several times almost lost life, due to pan-sexual assault and structural oppression of inequality, before, during, and after the life in Cornell as an undergraduate and graduate student.

With acute PTSD triggered by some recent waves of related social events as well as self-blaming on poor peer-reviewed placement at this stage of life, I went back to Cornell's sexual assult education pages to

  • debug what I had done wrong to be trapped and not recongnizing it,
  • regret (顾影自怜) on which opportunites I had missed then to rescue myself, but take this chance to do some re-self-educating
  • very confused about why evolved STEM-minded in very early life (not being the lazy girls) and went to prestigious schools had zero resisitance to ending up such a joke destiny,
  • questioned myself if I had done something ealier (personal rescue and professional contributions), could I have saved more lifes? Did I secondarily contributed this contagous chain? Did prepatrators and conspirators proudly used 'winning me' to position themselves as mentors in their PUA (pick up artist) tutorials or Nth room groups?
I noticed that Cornell is launching a task force on this issue. It's complex to distinguish
  • the confort that the school is serious about this issue and recognize her responsibility to piorneer world peace
  • anger that this had been non-stop
with the flashbacks (沉渣泛起). But I still tried to communicate this work to as a self comforting, professional blastering, if any, some previlage for communicating directly to executives with self protection, of being an Cornell alumni, compared to a random SMS post that sink into the information see then get frustrate or being judged by net trollers and cause secondary harm.

This work squeezes some remaining intellect and energy as an computer programmer (had almost been deprived with this single career opportunity as well) to address the sexual assualt topic under a computational point of view by metaphorize topics in computer science as well as promoting to use computational methods to research, educate, intervene sexual assult and debug the 'bug in the world equity' and bug in the life of the victims, prepatrators and people around us.

Highlighted contributions

  • Introduced cultural arbitraging under sexual assult context.
  • Point out the bride hunting culture among Chinese international student community.
  • Propose HCI (human computer interaction) ideas for SA prevention, advocacy and research.
  • Computational modeling on some SA settings.
  • Some analogy, such as bug, coalescing STEM and psychological, social intuitions.
    •

    Index

    • About the title and domain name
    • Cultural Arbitraging: an informal survey in terms of Chinese educational cycle #digital_humanity #text_mining #opinion_mining
      • Timeline of influentical events about sexual assult, especially in East Asia and/or educational environment #data_visualization
      • Bride hunting (选妃) cultures in Chinese international student community. Proved true.
      • A guaranteed path to graduate school (保研路). A myth among Chinese college students that the instituions often give a grad school ofter as mouth-shutting compensate.
      • Unnecessary helps as lures: between the gap of decoupling of how everyday life and study works. Degree of coworks cultural gap.
      • Following Yanjie Bian's theory on Chinese society. That people often believe that 'strong private connections' work as social capital, instead of 'weak professional connection'.
      • 文化大革命，矫枉过正的举报恐惧. 8964 no one walks. Private
      • Yellow-hair effect text mining
      • References
    • Modeling networks how many prepatrators on the reported victim number 23%: 260-560. Are they all reported?
    • On Terrence Tao's blue eye puzzle. Cascading behavior. Why researches, rules, common sense are always late and why we need them. #networks #cascading_behavior
    • Cycle of sexual assult.
    • Random computational and linguistic intuitions.
      • Baysian approach
      • Threats are always non-credible, but believing are always threatening
      • Dog whistling.
      • intuitionistic logic on psychological extreme. If it seems not ,it. Unless you believe. Evidence
      • Vulnerable bugs and how they might be programmed by the abuser or early envs.
      • experience as evidence. evidence as evidence
      • proof of always exists
      • misinformation
      • Cornell as a Galapagos

      • misinformation
    • Proposals on SA prevention with computational methods #hci #cscw
      • Using GenAI to moderate SA reporting, hearning and education process.
          Generate text image to visualized
      • Using GenAI to moderate SA reporting, hearning and education process.

    About the Title and Domain Name

    The title "Title IX: A Programmer's Perspective - Fantastic Preys and Where to Find Them", and the domain name of the site "debug your equality" draws inspiration from notable references:

    • Computer Systems: A Programmer's Perspective: This textbook is one of the most popular textboks among computer science students. This work metaphorize some computer science concepts, STEM jargons to the social context of sexual assult issue.
    • Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them: The 2016 movie about exploring magical creatures, paralleling our analysis on why 'fantastic preys' are vulnerable.
    • Debugging: Grace Hopper was the first woman programmer who first coined the term "bug" for computer system flaw, paralleling sexual assults and underlined incentives are the 'bugs' of peronal well-being and overall equality of our world, from a tech women's perspective.

    These themes highlight the intersection of technology, ethics, and education.

    On Terrence Tao's blue-eye island puzzle: why broadcasting and discussion are important and why some are responding late;

    The mathematician Terence Tao proposed the logic game blue-eyed island puzzle. Here I borrow the original text:

    There is an island upon which a tribe resides. The tribe consists of 1000 people, with various eye colours. Yet, their religion forbids them to know their own eye color, or even to discuss the topic; thus, each resident can (and does) see the eye colors of all other residents, but has no way of discovering his or her own (there are no reflective surfaces). If a tribesperson does discover his or her own eye color, then their religion compels them to commit ritual suicide at noon the following day in the village square for all to witness. All the tribespeople are highly logical and devout, and they all know that each other is also highly logical and devout (and they all know that they all know that each other is highly logical and devout, and so forth).

    Of the 1000 islanders, it turns out that 100 of them have blue eyes and 900 of them have brown eyes, although the islanders are not initially aware of these statistics (each of them can of course only see 999 of the 1000 tribespeople).

    One day, a blue-eyed foreigner visits to the island and wins the complete trust of the tribe.

    One evening, he addresses the entire tribe to thank them for their hospitality.

    However, not knowing the customs, the foreigner makes the mistake of mentioning eye color in his address, remarking “how unusual it is to see another blue-eyed person like myself in this region of the world”.

    What effect, if anything, does this faux pas have on the tribe?

    The key of the puzzle is the domino effect of "knowing everyone knows". Briefly prove by induction. Say the number of blue-eyed are N. When N=1, the only blue-eye see all the other's are brown eyed, then he knows he is the only blue-eyed, then he commits suicide. Now we prove by induction, every blue-eyed will see there are N-1 blue-eyed peers, if he is not blue-eyed, then the N-1 blue-eyed would commit suicide on day N-1. But he sees no other's committed suicide on day N-1, then he realizes he is blue-eyed. Symmetricly, all the N blue-eyed commit suicide. Why the foreign story teller can make a difference. The awareness of 'common knowledge' which is crutial in game theory setting that 'everyone knows that everyone already knows it'. The setting is similar to 'naked emperor' story by Anderson. Although everyone already know the emperor is naked, but not until the child say it loud, they aren't aware that 'everyone knows everyone knows'.

    How is it related to social setting of sexual assult?

    First, this reveals the importance of boardcasting. To make it publicly aware that SA, rape culture is not OK, we know that everyone knows, at least has the responsibility to know what's defined as sexual assult. If someone commits some misconduct, he has no excuse of 'not knowing' from cultural difference or so, nor the victim should make excuse for the prepatrator. If one risks to do this under the common awareness, he is a real dangerous person highly incentified of sexual power. Cornell's number This also reveals the importance of discussing. Reaction of sexual assualt are personal. Make it publicly aware that Furthermore, definitions, laws, scientific claims could be late. Trust your personal intuition. President Obama's Not Alone - Discussing and/or broadcasting makes difference. - curse of being highly logical. - The hazing number serves as a story teller. - Researches are late but we still need them. A final tragic remark, although it is the extreme mind game setting, suicide itself is the story teller. It is often the case that someone knows something and committed suicide, and not until the suicide that the others know about it (the remaining 200-N islanders are in.

    What I am proposing using this metaphor: - Some claims are oversimplified, ego, hyperat, not falsifiable, not scientific, are actually true but at the state before the story teller. - Social norms are quite local. They all have the information but not actually rendered. - There's lag in the cascading of 'suicide' during which arbitrage can happen. - Abstraction of 'all know' - A strong education on what's right or wrong, and acknowledge the time window, some 'bad' are bad, not your fault. - Suicide are story teller. - Information and belief.

    Cultural Arbitraging

    Section Intro

    The financial concept arbitraging, "buy low in one market" analouges scenarios where a prepatrator take advantage of cultural gap to gain sexual advantage. Jon Tillman's blog introduced 'cultural arbitraging' by the The cultural alphas(undervalued assets) include time window of information vacuum, social context difference, language gap, nostagia, uncertainty, etc. I provided pseudo-theoretical analysis and included discussion in Chinese social media.

    Key Events

    Year,Reported Brief Year,Event Prepatrator identity Age of prepatrator(event) Age of victim(event) Charge of prepatrator Notice about victim Opinion
    2025 dfa fdafldsajlkf djalf Maria Anders Germany Germany Germany Germany Germany Germany
    Centro comercial Moctezuma Francisco Chang Mexico Mexico Mexico Mexico Mexico Mexico Mexico

    A graranteed path to graduate school: A myth among Chinese colleges a direct grad school offer serves as a compensation of sexual assault.

    Too Late, But...

    About and FAQs

    On development

    • Q: Why unordered lists(i.e. bullet points instead of numerical section numbers) for all the lists this page?
      A: This page is subject to change. Ordinal bullet points referenced but changed later is a kind of gaslighting and may raise subtle mental confusion.
    • Q: Used genAI?
      A: Sure, used Claude for almost all web programming. I never took an introductory web design class officially and always not confident with my web works. Now genAI saved me a little. Color schema borrowed: 'Valspar’s Color of the Year: Encore'.

    On the contents

    • Q: Used GenAI?
      A: I used GenAI as theaurus and grammar corrector. Sometimes I used it to polish the style and then write on my own.
    • Q: Courses, researches and learning at Cornell inspired this work?
      A:
      • AEM 4190 Strategic Thinking with Prof. Nancy H. Chau, for game theory incentives especially on family dynamics, brain drain and peer competitions on Chinese intellectual immigrations and . Also hugely inspired by the book Jane Austen, a Game Theorist by Michael Suk-Young Chwe, which wakened us with a punch that the underpreviledge are also strategic agents in the game.
      • ORIE 6350 Game Theory and Mechanism Design with Prof. Krishnamurthy Iyer for rigorous math proofs, non-credible threat, mechanism design as the main source of behaviors, and 'nature as the player'.
      • INFO 6350, 3300 and RAship with INFO facaulty, including Prof. David Mimno, Prof. Daniel Cosley, Prof. Susan Fussell, Prof. Gilly Leshed for text mining as a method for public opinion mining, data visualization, human computer interaction, computer mediated communication especially on cross culture communication, and computational research on social topics
      • ORIE 3120 with Prof. Peter Jackson and Prof. Peter Frazier for data analysis, simulation
      • CS 6180 with Robert L. Constable for intuitionistic logic
      • CS 6850, 4820 for network theory and matching algorithms
      • CS 3110 with Michale Clarkson, CS 2800 with Michale Jorge and TAship with Prof. Jon Hopcroft, Prof. Joe Halpern, CS 2110 with Prof. David Gries, CS 4810 with Prof. Dexter Kozen thinking recursively, decision theory, computational complexity algos.
      • Prof. Robert H. Frank of Johnson Business school. I read the book The Economic Naturalist: In Search of Explanations for Everyday Enigmas when it first came to China in 2008 in and when I was a middle school student, this was the early awakener of the concepts like opportunity cost, sunk cost. Those saved my life. I once greeted him briefly when I stepped by his office.
    • Q: What happened during at Cornell?
    • Q: What happened these years?
    • Q: What are my factors to be a fantastic prey? A: Well, harsh question but I did go through them:
      • Q: What are the bugs?
      • Q: Why aren't those reported timely? A: That's why I have to write this, or the hurt would be non-terminating. Don't let this happen again. Or just make this happen less.
      • Q: Anyone else involved in this work? A: Not yet. Nor did I distribute the work to others although the domain is publicly available, i.e. can be cited.
      • Q: What to expect from the readers? A: The most desired things is professional critique and/or discussion on this work. Still, like a baby begging for mom's attention. Research collaboration will be surprisingly great.
      • Tragic, low self-esteem.

      © 2025 Title IX Initiative. All rights reserved. Please tolerate that web code is largely generated by GenAI.
